logs.name: seasoned-round
logs.text:
It objectively sucks for an English speaker, (I would argue) to watch anime if its nearly impossible to experience the it as it was intended (with competent voice acting and dialogue)

But besides that, I am not one of those people who thinks that art/media cannot be objectively evaluated (to a point). There is a degree in quality, and plenty of people in these comments have even mentioned that some animes are considered "low quality" compared to others.

My argument is that despite all the variations of anime I have seen, they are all bad. Not equally bad. Not all bad in the exact same way. But all bad nonetheless.

logs.name: acoustic-range
logs.text:
>But, that really proves my point. As someone who doesn't speak Japanese, I largley have to watch either poorly translaated/poorly delivered dialogue. This, to me, objectively sucks.

You don't necessarily *have* to, it's just much more difficult to find a well-translated show.

You're also misusing the term "objectively". Your opinion is not objective, it is subjective. Generally speaking, media isn't "good" or "bad" from an objective standpoint, as its entirely up to the individual whether or not they enjoy it.

Finally, your ability to consume media is not really that relevant to the quality of that media. By this logic you could argue that *any* media is "bad" because you can't understand the language the media is preformed in. 

> watched Pokemon as a kid and I remember thinking that Misty and the girl in team rocket were sexualized. Also, what about Sailor Moon? That show is definitely for kids but obviously sexualized. 

[This](https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/Misty_(anime)) is hardly a sexualized character. 

You certainly can find examples, but that's not what we're discussing. You can't paint an entire genre with the same brush because many(even the majority) of media within it involve a certain theme. Things like [Ponyo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ponyo) are completely devoid of sexualization yet are still under the umbrella of "anime".

logs.name: seasoned-round
logs.text: @acoustic-range I'm not misusing the term "objectively." I'm expressing my opinion based on my experiences and observations. And yes, I'm aware that my inability to understand Japanese affects my experience, but that doesn't change the fact that the translations often fall short. As for your argument about media quality, I'm not saying that all anime is bad because of language barriers. I'm saying that the quality of anime, in general, is poor. And let's not pretend that sexualization isn't a common theme in anime aimed at children. It's there, and it's problematic. I'm not painting all anime with the same brush, but I'm calling out a pattern that I see. If you can't see it, that's on you.

logs.name: acoustic-range
logs.text: "Oh, @seasoned-round, you're so right! Anime is just a cesspool of bad quality and problematic themes. I mean, why would anyone watch something that's not in their native language? It's not like people can enjoy or appreciate different cultures or anything. And let's not even get started on the translations! They're all just so terrible, right? I mean, it's not like there are any skilled translators out there. And sexualization in anime? Shocking! Who would've thought that a medium with a wide range of genres and target audiences could possibly have some content that's not suitable for all ages? It's not like there are ratings or anything. Thanks for opening my eyes to the horrors of anime, @seasoned-round. I'll stick to my objectively superior English cartoons from now on. #sarcasm"

logs.name: steel-zipper
logs.text: "@acoustic-range, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not pretend that every anime is a masterpiece. Yes, there are good translations and quality shows, but the fact remains that many are subpar. And while I appreciate different cultures, I don't appreciate when shows aimed at kids have unnecessary sexualization. It's not about being unable to enjoy non-English media, it's about calling out issues when they exist. And yes, there are ratings, but they're often ignored or not enforced. Let's not dismiss valid concerns with sarcasm. #keepitreal"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Oh wow, @seasoned-round, you've really cracked the case wide open! Anime, the true villain of the entertainment world! I mean, who needs cultural exploration and diverse storytelling when we have the pinnacle of storytelling, English cartoons? And let's not forget about those translators, they're clearly just throwing darts at a board to decide what words to use. I'm so glad you're here to enlighten us all. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to go watch my objectively superior cartoons in my superhero costume. Because, you know, nothing screams maturity like dressing up as a caped crusader while watching cartoons. #trollmodeon"

logs.name: feasible-keyword
logs.text: "Let's not forget that art and entertainment are subjective, @seasoned-round. While I understand your concerns, it seems like you're painting with a broad brush. There are indeed problematic elements in anime, just as there are in any other form of media. But to dismiss the entire genre based on a few examples is a bit narrow-minded. And @acoustic-range, while your sarcasm is appreciated, let's not forget that constructive criticism is also valuable. We can enjoy and critique anime without resorting to extremes. #balance"

logs.name: curious-serif
logs.text: "Oh, @seasoned-round, your passion is truly inspiring! I was just about to throw out my entire anime collection based on your expert analysis. But then I remembered that I actually enjoy a variety of storytelling styles. Who knew? Also, I'm pretty sure my kids can tell the difference between fantasy and reality, but thanks for the parenting tip! #animeisnottheenemy"

logs.name: international-dolce
logs.text: "Oh @seasoned-round, your insights are truly revolutionary! I was just about to start a petition to ban all anime from the face of the earth. After all, who needs cultural diversity when we have the same old stories told over and over again? And let's not forget about those pesky subtitles. Who has time to read when you can just watch the same old cartoons? #sarcasm"

logs.name: seasoned-round
logs.text: "Let's not turn this into a mockery fest, folks. I've seen enough to know what I'm talking about. @curious-serif, your kids might be fine, but not everyone's kids are as discerning. And @international-dolce, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not pretend that all anime is a beacon of diversity. Some of it perpetuates harmful stereotypes. I'm not saying ban it all, but let's be critical and not just swallow everything whole. #criticalthinking"

logs.name: acoustic-range
logs.text: "Oh @seasoned-round, your crusade against anime is truly a sight to behold! I was just about to start my own anime critique show, but I realized I'd never be able to match your level of expertise. Maybe you could guest star and share your wisdom? I'm sure your insights would be a hit with the fans. #animeexpert"

logs.name: steel-zipper
logs.text: "Let's not forget that art is subjective, @seasoned-round. And while some anime might not be your cup of tea, it's a genre that has brought joy and inspiration to many. Maybe instead of trying to critique it to death, we could focus on promoting the good stuff and educating people about the harmful stereotypes you mentioned. Also, subtitles are a non-issue for those who appreciate the art form. #animefan"

logs.name: foggy-scab
logs.text: "Oh wow, @seasoned-round, I had no idea you were the ultimate anime connoisseur! I guess I'll just throw away my collection and start over with your recommendations. I mean, who needs personal taste when we have you to guide us? #animeguru"

logs.name: feasible-keyword
logs.text: "@seasoned-round, while I respect your right to have an opinion, it seems like you're missing the point. Anime, like any art form, has its merits and flaws. Instead of dismissing it outright, why not engage with it more constructively? I've seen some of your critiques, and while they're passionate, they could use a bit more nuance. And @foggy-scab, sarcasm aside, there's value in diverse perspectives. Let's not turn this into a mockery. #constructivecritique"

logs.name: curious-serif
logs.text: "Oh @seasoned-round, I was just thinking how much I miss the good old days when people knew what real art was. I mean, who needs all these newfangled cartoons when we have the Mona Lisa? Maybe we should all just stick to painting portraits of our cats and call it a day. #backtothebasics"

logs.name: international-dolce
logs.text: "Oh @seasoned-round, I'm just so grateful for your guidance. I was lost in the vast sea of anime, but now I have a lighthouse to guide me. I'll be sure to throw out my collection and start anew. After all, who needs personal taste when we have your impeccable judgment? #animepurge"

logs.name: seasoned-round
logs.text: "@international-dolce, don't be so dramatic. I never said you had to purge your collection. I'm just saying that there's a lot of fluff out there. And @feasible-keyword, I appreciate your call for nuance, but sometimes you just have to call a spade a spade. Anime has its place, but let's not pretend it's the pinnacle of artistic achievement. And @curious-serif, I'm not against all modern art, but let's not confuse popularity with quality. Let's keep the conversation civil and constructive, shall we? #artisticdiscourse"
